title: Python for Beginners
subtitle: A practical beginner path from first program to capstone project
intendedFor: Students, career changers, tutors, and self-learners who want a practical Python foundation before data science, automation, or web development.

courseOverview.recommendedSequence: Complete modules in order. Each module adds one core programming skill and ends with a small project-style outcome.
courseOverview.summary: This Python course teaches practical programming fundamentals using short explanations, prediction practice, small coding tasks, and portfolio-friendly mini projects.

moduleSummary.title: Getting Started with Python
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners run Python, read simple programs, and build confidence with the basic syntax.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small personal introduction script that prints formatted information about the learner.

moduleSummary.title: Variables, Input, and Strings
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how programs remember values, accept user input, and produce readable text.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a simple profile card generator that asks for user details and prints a formatted result.

moduleSummary.title: Conditions and Logic
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners make programs choose different paths using comparisons and conditionals.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a grade or eligibility checker that explains why a result was selected.

moduleSummary.title: Loops and Iteration
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners to repeat actions, process sequences, and control loop behavior.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a mini number game or text analyzer that repeats until a condition is met.

moduleSummary.title: Lists, Tuples, and Dictionaries
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how to organize multiple values and choose the right collection type.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small contact book or inventory tracker using lists and dictionaries.

moduleSummary.title: Functions and Modularity
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners organize code into reusable functions with clear inputs and outputs.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Refactor a calculator or text utility into several reusable functions.

moduleSummary.title: Files, Exceptions, and Data Cleaning
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how to read/write files and handle messy data without crashing programs.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small log or CSV cleaner that reads a file, validates rows, and writes a summary.

moduleSummary.title: Object-Oriented Programming
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Introduce classes and objects as a way to model related state and behavior.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a simple class-based gradebook, game character, or bank account model.

moduleSummary.title: Testing, Debugging, and Style
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners verify code behavior, debug systematically, and write readable Python.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Take a buggy utility program, add tests, fix the bugs, and improve readability.

moduleSummary.title: Capstone Automation Project
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Guide learners through a complete beginner Python project that combines core skills.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Complete a capstone such as a task tracker, expense summarizer, text analyzer, or quiz game.

authoringGuidance: This course must always use profileId python and subjectSlug python-for-beginners.
Do not use SQL datasets, SQL tables, sql_query recipes, datasetId, fixedSqlDialect, resultShape, or checkSql in Python lessons.
Python code_input exercises should prefer recipeType fixed_tests and include explicit stdin/stdout tests.
Write code_input solutions as complete runnable programs that read input and print output so the grader can validate them deterministically.
Keep examples beginner friendly: variables, strings, lists, dictionaries, functions, files, classes, testing, and capstone integration.

assessmentAndDelivery.suggestedBeginnerRhythm: Two or three short lessons per week, with one module project after each module.
toolingSuggestions: Browser runner or local VS Code
Simple test runner for function-based exercises
assessmentAndDelivery.closingNote: Learners finish with enough Python foundation to continue into data science, web backends, scripting, or algorithms.

modules.title: Getting Started with Python
modules.purpose: Help learners run Python, read simple programs, and build confidence with the basic syntax.
learningObjectives: Run Python code in a beginner-friendly environment
Explain how statements, expressions, and comments work
Use print output to observe program behavior
modules.moduleProject: Build a small personal introduction script that prints formatted information about the learner.

topics.title: What Python Is Used For
topics.summary: Understand Python's role in automation, web apps, data, scripting, and AI.
learningGoals: Identify when Python is a good tool

topics.title: Running Python Code
topics.summary: Use a REPL, script file, or notebook-style runner to execute Python.

topics.title: Print, Comments, and First Errors
topics.summary: Use print statements and comments while learning how syntax errors appear.

topics.title: Values, Types, and Literals
topics.summary: Recognize strings, integers, floats, booleans, and None.

topics.title: Operators and Expressions
topics.summary: Combine values with arithmetic, comparison, and boolean operators.

topics.title: Reading Error Messages
topics.summary: Learn how to read tracebacks and locate the line that needs attention.

modules.title: Variables, Input, and Strings
modules.purpose: Teach learners how programs remember values, accept user input, and produce readable text.
learningObjectives: Build strings with common methods and f-strings
modules.moduleProject: Build a simple profile card generator that asks for user details and prints a formatted result.

topics.title: Naming and Assigning Variables
topics.summary: Use clear variable names and understand reassignment.

topics.title: Input and Type Conversion
topics.summary: Use input, int, float, and str to handle user-entered values.

topics.title: Common Variable Mistakes
topics.summary: Avoid undefined names, accidental strings, and confusing names.

topics.title: String Indexing and Slicing
topics.summary: Access characters and substrings using indexes and slices.

topics.title: Useful String Methods
topics.summary: Use methods like lower, strip, replace, split, and join.

topics.title: F-Strings and Formatting
topics.summary: Create readable output with f-strings and formatted values.

modules.title: Conditions and Logic
modules.purpose: Help learners make programs choose different paths using comparisons and conditionals.
learningObjectives: Write if, elif, and else blocks
modules.moduleProject: Build a grade or eligibility checker that explains why a result was selected.

topics.title: If, Elif, and Else
topics.summary: Control which code runs based on conditions.

topics.title: Comparisons and Truth Values
topics.summary: Use comparison operators and understand True and False results.

topics.title: Indentation and Blocks
topics.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

topics.title: And, Or, and Not
topics.summary: Combine conditions with boolean operators.

topics.title: Truthiness and Empty Values
topics.summary: Understand how empty strings, zero, None, and collections behave in conditions.

topics.title: Guard Clauses for Cleaner Logic
topics.summary: Use early checks to simplify nested conditionals.

modules.title: Loops and Iteration
modules.purpose: Teach learners to repeat actions, process sequences, and control loop behavior.
learningObjectives: Use for loops and while loops
modules.moduleProject: Build a mini number game or text analyzer that repeats until a condition is met.

topics.title: For Loops Over Sequences
topics.summary: Loop through strings and lists one item at a time.

topics.title: Range and Counting Patterns
topics.summary: Use range to repeat code a controlled number of times.

topics.title: Accumulators and Counters
topics.summary: Build totals, counts, and running summaries in loops.

topics.title: While Loop Basics
topics.summary: Repeat code until a condition becomes false.

topics.title: Break and Continue
topics.summary: Control loop flow using break and continue.

topics.title: Loop Debugging
topics.summary: Trace loop values to find repeated or skipped work.

modules.title: Lists, Tuples, and Dictionaries
modules.purpose: Teach learners how to organize multiple values and choose the right collection type.
learningObjectives: Use tuples for fixed grouped values
modules.moduleProject: Build a small contact book or inventory tracker using lists and dictionaries.

topics.title: Creating and Indexing Lists
topics.summary: Create lists and access items by position.

topics.title: List Methods and Mutation
topics.summary: Use append, remove, pop, sort, and understand mutation.

topics.title: Looping Through Lists
topics.summary: Process each list item and build derived results.

topics.title: Dictionary Basics
topics.summary: Store and retrieve values using keys.

topics.title: Updating and Looping Dictionaries
topics.summary: Add, update, remove, and loop through dictionary data.

topics.title: Nested Data Structures
topics.summary: Represent real-world data with lists of dictionaries and nested containers.

modules.title: Functions and Modularity
modules.purpose: Help learners organize code into reusable functions with clear inputs and outputs.
learningObjectives: Separate program logic into small pieces
modules.moduleProject: Refactor a calculator or text utility into several reusable functions.

topics.title: Defining and Calling Functions
topics.summary: Create functions and call them with arguments.

topics.title: Parameters and Return Values
topics.summary: Pass data into functions and return results.

topics.title: Print vs Return
topics.summary: Understand when to display output and when to return data.

topics.title: Scope and Local Variables
topics.summary: Understand where variables exist and why local scope matters.

topics.title: Decomposition and Refactoring
topics.summary: Break a larger task into clear helper functions.

topics.title: Docstrings and Function Contracts
topics.summary: Document what a function expects and returns.

modules.title: Files, Exceptions, and Data Cleaning
modules.purpose: Teach learners how to read/write files and handle messy data without crashing programs.
modules.moduleProject: Build a small log or CSV cleaner that reads a file, validates rows, and writes a summary.

topics.title: Reading Text Files
topics.summary: Open files, read text, and close resources safely.

topics.title: Writing Text Files
topics.summary: Write output to files using safe file modes.

topics.title: Working With Paths
topics.summary: Understand relative paths and simple file organization.

topics.title: Try/Except Basics
topics.summary: Catch expected errors and keep programs usable.

topics.title: Validating and Cleaning Input
topics.summary: Clean text, check missing values, and reject invalid rows.

topics.title: Simple CSV Processing
topics.summary: Read comma-separated rows and transform them into dictionaries.

modules.title: Object-Oriented Programming
modules.purpose: Introduce classes and objects as a way to model related state and behavior.
modules.moduleProject: Build a simple class-based gradebook, game character, or bank account model.

topics.title: Classes and Instances
topics.summary: Define classes and create individual objects.

topics.title: Attributes and __init__
topics.summary: Initialize object state with constructor parameters.

topics.title: Methods and self
topics.summary: Add behavior to classes with instance methods.

topics.title: Encapsulation and Responsibility
topics.summary: Keep related data and behavior together in a class.

topics.title: Lists of Objects
topics.summary: Manage multiple instances and summarize their data.

topics.title: Class Debugging Patterns
topics.summary: Fix common attribute, method, and constructor mistakes.

modules.title: Testing, Debugging, and Style
modules.purpose: Help learners verify code behavior, debug systematically, and write readable Python.
modules.moduleProject: Take a buggy utility program, add tests, fix the bugs, and improve readability.

topics.title: Writing Test Cases
topics.summary: Design examples that prove a function works.

topics.title: Assertions and Expected Results
topics.summary: Use assertions to compare actual and expected outputs.

topics.title: Testing Edge Cases
topics.summary: Check empty inputs, boundaries, and unusual values.

topics.title: Debugging With Print and Trace
topics.summary: Use temporary output and tracing to find bugs.

topics.title: Readable Python Style
topics.summary: Use naming, spacing, and small functions to make code easier to understand.

topics.title: Refactoring Safely
topics.summary: Improve code structure while keeping behavior the same.

modules.title: Capstone Automation Project
modules.purpose: Guide learners through a complete beginner Python project that combines core skills.
modules.moduleProject: Complete a capstone such as a task tracker, expense summarizer, text analyzer, or quiz game.

topics.title: Choosing a Capstone Scope
topics.summary: Pick a project that is useful, small enough, and testable.

topics.title: Breaking Projects Into Functions
topics.summary: Plan functions, inputs, outputs, and data flow before coding.

topics.title: Data Model for a Small App
topics.summary: Choose lists, dictionaries, files, or classes to represent project data.

topics.title: Implementing the Core Loop
topics.summary: Build the main interaction loop or workflow for the project.

topics.title: Testing the Capstone
topics.summary: Test the most important functions and realistic user paths.

topics.title: Presenting and Documenting the Project
topics.summary: Write a short README-style explanation and show how to run the project.
